meta.fun_fact: Irish Hills sits just outside the village of Carp, ON — home to the Diefenbunker, Canada's Cold War nuclear bunker. Built in 1959 to shelter 535 government officials, it was kept secret for decades and is now a national museum.

reviews.text: Greens were terrible — like putting on gravel. Not sure who is maintaining them but they need a serious upgrade.
Showed up for our tee time and they had double-booked the slot. Staff shrugged. Somehow still charged us a cancellation fee.
Course conditions were shocking for the price. Fairways were patchy, divots everywhere, and the cart path nearly destroyed my suspension.
Tee boxes are basically dirt patches at this point. Paid full price to hit off what felt like a construction site.
In the Spring, the mosquitos and blackflies can be thick. Spent more time swatting than swinging.
Some fairways need top dressing badly. Inconsistent conditions throughout — you'll have a great hole then a disaster.

stops.name: Carp Custom Creamery
stops.desc: Local institution. Real cream, huge scoops, unique flavours like Skoreo, raspberry cheesecake, and banoffee pie. People drive 40km just for this. Milkshakes too.

stops.name: Carp Bakery
stops.desc: Family-run bakery right in the village. Legendary cinnamon buns — light, fresh, and barely sweet. Sourdough that gets shipped back to BC. Show up early before they sell out.

stops.name: Skylark Café
stops.desc: Perfect 5.0 rating. Cosy, welcoming café right in Carp village. Handmade tables, great coffee, homemade sandwiches and gluten-free treats. The owner made every detail herself.

stops.name: Alice's Village Café
stops.desc: Beloved local spot with 1,371 reviews. The big cinnamon bun is not to be missed. Fresh soups, flatbreads, and salads. Closes at 3pm so time it right after the round.

stops.name: VANILLE Café & Bakery
stops.desc: New family-owned café on Stittsville Main. French pastries, almond croissants, macarons. The pistachio royal latte and honey almond latte are standouts.

scorecards.course: Timber Run Golf Club & Event Lodge

meta.fun_fact: Château Cartier Golf Course opened in 1923 and sits on the banks of the Ottawa River, offering views of the Champlain Bridge rapids and Parliament Hill's Peace Tower. It's considered one of Canada's best downtown golf resort experiences.

reviews.text: Fairways lacking grass, tee boxes essentially no grass and hard to place your tee, greens not well kept, bumpy. Same high-end price though!
Half of the yardage markers on the tees are facing the wrong way, and some holes almost seem to overlap. Dangerous.
The condos have taken over! The first and 18th holes are terrible. Hole 18 is somewhere around 50-60 yards long.
I am always concerned before crossing the road along the river to play the 4th hole as car traffic is important and people drive like maniacs.
The folks at Cartier need to take a lesson in customer service and get their business straightened out with Chrono Golf. Avoid this golf course.
Ok but not in great shape. There are a lot of overlapping holes which I think is dangerous. A few interesting holes but too many dull ones.

stops.name: Boulangerie Aux Deux Frères
stops.desc: 4.9 stars from 474 reviews. Best croissants and chocolatines in Aylmer. Small place, fresh baked daily — get there before they sell out.

stops.name: Maison Oddo
stops.desc: One of the best patisseries in the Ottawa-Gatineau region. A French diplomat reportedly drives 25 minutes every day just for the croissants. Borderline unreasonable.

stops.name: les cafés Aroma Select
stops.desc: Best coffee in the NCR according to regulars. Freshly roasted and ground on-site. Staff match you to the perfect roast. Also carries tea, spices, and chocolate.

stops.name: Café Mulligan
stops.desc: Cosy locally owned café with heart and soul. Exceptional mochas, fresh food, gluten-free options. Closes at 3pm — head straight there after the round.

stops.name: Chocolats Favoris
stops.desc: Specialty chocolate-dipped ice cream. The dip is absurdly thick — get the cone filled with fondant at the bottom for the full experience. Order a small.

stops.name: Fidélice – Artisan gourmet
stops.desc: Stunning artisan cakes and pastries. The chocolate mousse cake Évasion is legendary. Gluten-free options available. Worth the slightly longer drive.
